channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A driver rams an anti-government rally in Serbia's capital and injures one protester
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — A woman rammed a car into a crowd of anti-government protesters in Serbia's capital and injured one of them Friday, police said, as a student-led strike shut down businesses and drew tens of thousands of people to demonstrations around the country.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the escalating conflict in eastern Congo as rebels close in on Goma
item.description: Fighting has escalated sharply in recent weeks in eastern Congo, where rebels have seized key towns and are closing in on the city of Goma, the government’s last stronghold in the North Kivu province, where Goma is the provincial capital and which borders Rwanda.
item.dc:creator: Monika Pronczuk And Mark Banchereau, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump had ‘fiery’ call with Danish prime minister over Greenland: Report
item.description: President Trump reportedly held a “fiery” call with Denmark’s Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen over the president’s insistence that U.S. control of Greenland is necessary for American national security.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hamas releases names of 4 female hostages to be released next, possibly violates deal
item.description: Hamas says it will release four female IDF soldiers including Karina Ariev, Daniella Gilboa, Naama Levy and Liri Albag in second hostage release in a possible violation of the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel’s military will remain in Lebanon as ceasefire deadline nears
item.description: Israel said its forces will remain in southern Lebanon after UN and Lebanese forces moved too slowly under the truce with Hezbollah established in November, which remains unfulfilled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tens of thousands protest in Slovakia over pro-Russia policies of populist leader Fico
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people in Slovakia gathered at squares and street across the country on Friday to voice their opposition to the pro-Russia policies of populist Prime Minister Robert Fico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China's Lunar New Year travel rush kicks into high gear with 9 billion trips expected
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China’s Lunar New Year travel rush has kicked into high gear, with billions of trips expected in coming days for the peak of the 40-day annual mass migration — the world's biggest annual movement of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Soprano Lise Davidsen pregnant with twins, cancels performances from mid-March through December
item.description: Soprano Lise Davidsen is pregnant with twins and is canceling her performances from mid-March through the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea prepares to send more troops to Russia after suffering casualties, South Korea says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s military said Friday it suspects North Korea is preparing to send additional troops to Russia after its soldiers fighting in the Russian-Ukraine war suffered heavy casualties.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin echoes Trump's claim that conflict in Ukraine could have been avoided had he been in office
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has echoed U.S. President Donald Trump’s claim the conflict in Ukraine could have been prevented had he been in the White House in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taliban reject court move to arrest top officials for persecuting Afghan women and girls
item.description: The Taliban Friday rejected a court move to arrest two of their top officials for persecuting women, accusing the court of baseless accusations and misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: 4 Israeli women soldiers will be next hostages freed from Gaza
item.description: The second batch of Israeli hostages set to be released by Hamas this weekend are four female soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Major storm lashes Ireland and Scotland as hurricane-force winds down power lines and ground flights
item.description: Millions of people in Ireland and northern parts of the U.K. have heeded the advice of authorities to stay at home.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Freedom is bittersweet for Palestinians released from Israeli jails
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — When Dania Hanatsheh was released from an Israeli jail this week and dropped off by bus into a sea of jubilant Palestinians in Ramallah, it was an uncomfortable déjà vu.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine is reforming its recruitment efforts to attract younger soldiers and boost forces
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine is in the final stages of drafting recruitment reforms to attract 18- to 25-year-olds who are currently exempt from mobilization as it looks for ways to bolster its fighting force, the battlefield commander recently appointed to the President’s Office said.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC prosecutor requests warrants for Afghan Taliban leaders over persecution of women
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor announced on Thursday he had requested arrest warrants for two top Afghan Taliban officials for the repression of women.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: M23 rebels battling Congo's army close in on Goma as panic spreads among city's 2 million people
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Panic spread in eastern Congo's main city on Thursday, with M23 rebels steadily inching closer to Goma and seizing a nearby town as they battle the Congolese army.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haitians demand priority after government spends $3.8M for a brief visit from Colombia's leader
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — The money materialized as soon as Colombia’s president confirmed his visit to southern Haiti.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scholz's challenger vows tough border rules as attack puts migration in German election spotlight
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Germany's opposition leader vowed Thursday to bar people from entering the country without proper papers and to step up deportations if he is elected chancellor next month, as a knife attack by a rejected asylum-seeker spilled over into an election campaign in which he is the front-run
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Davos 2025: Some notable quotes from the World Economic Forum meeting
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — Leading business and political figures attending the World Economic Forum meeting in Davos, Switzerland, have discussed and debated topics such as technology, tariffs, climate change, Ukraine, Gaza and the global economy this week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House pair introduces bill to end China's preferred trade status
item.description: Legislation to revoke China’s Permanent Normal Trade Relations was introduced Thursday by a bipartisan pair of House lawmakers, building on a Republican effort last year to repeal Beijing’s preferential trade status. The bill would solidify efforts by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump picks former fast-food chain CEO Andrew Puzder for EU ambassador
item.description: President Trump has selected Andrew Puzder, a former fast-food chain CEO who he attempted to install in his first White House Cabinet, as his pick for ambassador to the European Union. Trump praised Puzder, the former chief of CKE restaurants, as steering fast-food chains like Carl’s Jr.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Saudi crown prince says kingdom intends to invest $600 billion in US during call with Trump
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s crown prince says the kingdom wants to invest $600 billion in the United States over the next four years, comments that came after President Donald Trump mused about returning to the kingdom as his first foreign trip.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump talks tough with Putin on ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump is turning up the heat on Russian President Vladimir Putin in his first days in power, seeking to pressure Russia’s leader to come to the negotiating table for a deal to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump redesignates Houthis as foreign terrorist organization
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday directed his administration to relist the Houthis in Yemen as a foreign terrorist organization (FTO) in an effort to crack down on the Iranian-backed militant group.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: More than 80 people were killed in northeast Colombia over the weekend after the government failed to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin, Xi vow to ‘deepen’ alliance hours after Trump re-enters the White House
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and Russian President Vladimir Putin held a video call hours after President Donald Trump re-entered the White House and pledged to "deepen" their partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Stray dog brings sick, freezing puppy to vet's office
item.description: A stray dog was caught on camera carrying her sick puppy to the front door of a veterinarian clinic in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC
